key_insight: The paper turns a coupled Stokes-Biot fluid–poroelastic problem into a scalable interface-only system using Lagrange multipliers and Schur complements, enabling parallel subdomain solves.
engineering_relevance: This provides a practical domain decomposition framework for large-scale simulations of fluid–structure–porous media interaction in biomechanics and geomechanics.
method: Non-overlapping decomposition into Stokes and Biot subdomains with Stokes-Stokes, Biot-Biot, and Stokes-Biot interfaces.
Enforce interface physics via Lagrange multipliers for velocity, displacement, and pressure continuity, including Beavers-Joseph-Saffman slip.
Reduce the global mixed finite element system to a GMRES-solved interface problem with local subdomain solves in parallel.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports theoretical positive-definiteness and fields-of-value convergence analysis, supported by numerical experiments demonstrating solver performance.
watch_item: Performance depends on stable mixed finite element discretizations and the quality of the interface formulation, so implementation details and preconditioning are likely critical.
paper_title: Domain decomposition methods for the Stokes-Biot model of fluid-poroelastic structure interaction
